# Agent Machines

> OpenRouter for agents and containers.

## Summary

Agent Machines is the **product layer** above sandboxes: a control plane that deploys a full persistent agent worker in one unit — runtime, skills, MCP, integrations, cron, observation, and fleet management — on any substrate.

**Analogies:** OpenRouter for agents **and** containers (route runtime + substrate in one account). Vercel on AWS for substrates (product layer vs interchangeable infra).

**Fleet:** Provision design, news, code, or ops agents from opinionated presets (Hermes, OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex). Vendor SKUs are UI + skills + MCPs + prompts; we ship that composable, one click per specialist, one dashboard for the fleet.

**Providers:** E2B, Sprites.dev, Dedalus Machines (interchangeable). Dedalus benchmarks best on boot/sleep in our harness — strong default, not the product.

Open source (MIT): <https://github.com/Kevin-Liu-01/agent-machines>

## Core facts

- **Product:** dual routing + persistent workers + fleet supervision — not VM hosting, not chat sessions
- **Persistence:** `~/.agent-machines/` on `/home/machine` — chats, skills, cron, sessions, MCP config
- **Agents:** Hermes, OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex CLI
- **Harness** (registry-derived — `web/lib/platform/harness.ts`):
  - **161** SKILL.md skills
  - **27** service routes · **35** MCP servers · **23+** CLIs
  - **9–23** agent-native tools by runtime
- **Observation:** activity, sessions, tool calls, logs, cost
- **Roadmap:** MCP/CLI for agent-to-agent fleet orchestration

## Important pages
